item.title: NASA-Funded Study Shows How Controlled Burns Help Sequoias Survive Wildfires - NASA Science
item.description: A new study, funded by NASA, found that giant sequoias were nearly four times more likely to survive extreme wildfires in forests that have been managed with prescribed burns, which decrease tree mortality.

item.title: WMO RA VI expands Services and Applications Training and Resource Toolkit with new edition focused on data resources
item.description: The WMO Regional Association VI (RA VI) has released the second edition of the Services and Applications Training and Resource Toolkit, focusing on data resources supporting weather, climate, hydrological and environmental services.Building on the first edition, the new toolkit provides a curated guide to selected data platforms, repositories and visualization tools to help NMHSs, researchers and partners discover, access and use relevant observations, climate datasets, hydrological information and environmental data.

item.title: Utah Helicopter Flights Test NASA’s DAVINCI Mission to Venus - NASA Science
item.description: To prepare their spacecraft for Venus, NASA's DAVINCI scientists traveled to the most Venus-like place on Earth.

item.title: Lunar Science Stories - NASA Science
item.description: As NASA prepares for a sustained human presence on the Moon, missions will increasingly require spacecraft that can navigate and communicate without a direct connection to Earth. NASA’s Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment, or CAPSTONE, validated…

item.title: Information for Journalists
item.description: Do not hesitate to contact WMO Strategic Communications Office if you want to:Ask a questionTalk to an expertRequest an interview Press conferencesPress conferences and other media events are announced in the Event section.Embargoed materialTo receive press releases in your e-mail, please register on the Media Releases page.All press releases are sent under embargo. Please respect the time and date of public release. ContactsClare Nullis, Media Officer, cnullis at wmo.int - +41797091397

item.title: Events and Meetings
item.description: The results will automatically refresh as filter values change.

item.title: Press Releases
item.description: Welcome to the WMO Media Centre!If you are a registered media, a partner organization or a member of the WMO community, you can subscribe to receive our press releases under embargo. For any media query, please send an e-mail to media at wmo.int

item.title: Can we stop El Niño before it starts? | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: A controversial geoengineering proposal suggests that brightening clouds off South America could weaken a burgeoning El Niño, but major technical and ethical questions remain.

item.title: New eDNA tool to help track recovery of sunflower sea star, a Pacific Coast 'apex predator' | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: A wasting disease that surged during the intense 2013-2016 Pacific marine heatwave known as the Blob decimated numerous species of sea stars. Researchers at NOAA's Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory (PMEL) have developed a rapid environmental DNA (eDNA) detection method that dramatically improves scientists' ability to monitor the sea star species' health and recovery.

item.title: NOAA tests new ocean drone in Lake Washington to better understand atmospheric rivers | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: If you've seen a robotic boat cruising around Lake Washington, it may have been NOAA's Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory (PMEL) testing a new ocean drone. It's all to understand atmospheric rivers better.

item.title: Here's what a super El Niño could mean for the climate crisis | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: Scientists are still untangling the complex relationship between El Niño and human-driven warming, but their confluence has major implications for extreme weather and global temperatures.

item.title: A powerful El Niño is forming. If history is a guide, it could hit hard. | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: The biggest episodes of the past have altered the course of human events, according to researchers. An emerging one is drawing historic comparisons.

item.title: Another ocean drone joins NOAA’s growing fleet | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: It’s bright yellow, solar-powered and self-propelled. It can stay at sea for months, collecting data with a wide variety of off-the-shelf and NOAA-designed instruments. Meet the SeaTrac.

item.title: Fund for Science and Technology Awards $15 Million to Scripps Oceanography | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: PMEL/GOBOP, Scripps Ocean Institute and Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution received the Fund for Science and Technology Award from Paul G. Allen Philanthropies for expanding Deep Argo float research.

item.title: New compendium opens access to 47 years of data on Alaska waters | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: Understanding how Alaska’s vast marine ecosystems are changing requires long, consistent records — something scientists have rarely had access to in one place.

item.title: Tracking algae and climate change from the sky with Arctic AIR | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: A specialized plane, camera and a crew of four are in Alaska to understand the ecosystems of the Bering and Chukchi seas. UW CICOES / NOAA PMEL scientist Jiaxu Zhang, the ArcticAIR project lead, is quoted.

item.title: Cooperation, not conflict on the High Seas | Pacific Marine Environmental Laboratory
item.description: [Podcast] Today on Future Tense on ABC Radio National, we'll look at the progress of two ocean-related initiatives, efforts where nations, non-governed organizations, and companies, are coming together to build a framework for how we regulate and protect the oceans.

item.title: Will the promise of rain threaten the sun’s long reign?
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: Marine heatwave intensified impacts of Storm Babet by 9-20%, new study finds
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: Climate extremes are becoming the new ‘normal’ for the UK
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: Heatwave continues with little rain in the forecast
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: More than 2,700 excess deaths estimated in England and Wales during May and June heatwaves
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: New research shows AI can produce trustworthy, physically realistic weather forecasts
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: Weather intelligence has the power to help people plan great days out, say weather and tourism chiefs
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: Heatwave continues as UK records unprecedented run of 35°C days
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: Prolonged spell of hot weather continues into next week
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: Third heatwave of year to bring prolonged spell of hot and dry weather
item.description: Author: Met Office

item.title: Machine learning opens new opportunities for global reanalysis
item.description: A prototype machine-learning reanalysis trained directly on observations reconstructs more than four decades of atmospheric conditions, opening new possibilities for future reanalysis production.

item.title: ECMWF opens Call for Proposals for SEWA regional pilots on impact-based forecasting in Africa
item.description: Regional pilot projects will help strengthen preparedness for hazardous weather and climate-related events across Africa.

item.title: ECMWF data used in landmark global study that tracks climate hazards facing children
item.description: ERA5 data contributes to new UNICEF report mapping children’s exposure to climate hazards worldwide, supporting more informed risk assessment and decision-making.

item.title: ECMWF launches earthkit 1.0 for next-generation weather and climate workflows
item.description: With earthkit 1.0, ECMWF is making it easier for users to access, analyse and visualise weather and climate data, supporting better services and decision-making.

item.title: Irina Sandu takes up the role of Deputy Director-General and Director of Science and Innovation
item.description: Irina Sandu takes up a new leadership role, bringing more than 20 years of scientific and operational experience to help shape ECMWF’s future research and innovation.

item.title: Destination Earth moves into its third phase
item.description: Destination Earth enters its third phase, evolving its digital twins and AI to deliver integrated, user-focused capabilities for understanding and preparing for climate change and extreme events.

item.title: ECMWF Council highlights progress on forecasting, resilience and future capability
item.description: The ECMWF Council discussed future priorities, including collaboration with Member States, advances in forecasting and AI, investments in computing, and progress towards a more connected European meteorological infrastructure.

item.title: Computing power is the backbone for sustaining world-leading weather forecasts
item.description: ECMWF highlights strategic importance of high-performance computing at the ISC High Performance conference.

item.title: World first as ECMWF brings cloud radar data into its global forecasting system
item.description: ECMWF becomes the first weather forecasting centre to assimilate cloud radar data, improving how clouds are represented in global forecasts.

item.title: ECMWF Annual Report 2025 published
item.description: The ECMWF Annual Report 2025 highlights a year of significant achievements, including the operationalisation of the AIFS, the opening of the Real-time Catalogue, and ECMWF’s 50th anniversary.

item.title: Big ideas: How to make the global energy system more sustainable
item.description: Climate change scientist Debora Ley discusses how the world can break its addiction to fossil fuels and give more people access to clean, reliable electricity.

item.title: Shoot for the Moon: Can space exploration be sustainable?
item.description: As lunar exploration is regaining momentum, the sustainability of space launches has come into the spotlight.

item.title: Can nature win the next World Cup? Lessons from 2026 host cities
item.description: As billions of fans around the globe follow the FIFA World Cup 2026, several host cities are using the tournament as an opportunity to accelerate the green transition and protect nature.

item.title: I live in Paris. During heatwaves, the City of Light dims.
item.description: A heatwave blanketing France is the latest sign that we must rethink how our cities are designed and how our buildings are constructed.

item.title: As land is degrading, sand storms wreak havoc on desert communities
item.description: The powerful tempests are becoming increasingly common as climate change and land degradation turn once-productive landscapes into desert.

item.title: Earth has two football pitches of land for every human. Much of it is ailing.
item.description: Climate change and destructive practices, like deforestation and agricultural sprawl, have left 40 per cent of Earth’s land degraded.

item.title: The hidden environmental cost of your jeans – and what’s being done about it
item.description: Supermodel and UNEP Goodwill Ambassador Amber Valletta travels to Tunisia, where denim manufacturers are striving to lighten their environmental footprint.

item.title: Nine ways to stay cool during a heatwave
item.description: From drawing your curtains to placing wet towels on "pulse points", these tips will help you contend with spiking temperatures, which are becoming more common.

item.title: Restoring salmon, the “king of fish”, to Canada’s rivers and creeks
item.description: Atlantic salmon are returning to Canada’s rivers thanks to an Indigenous-led restoration effort that is reviving ecosystems, cultural traditions and endangered fish populations.

item.title: News — IPCC
item.description: News — IPCC

item.title: 2027 IPCC Methodology Report on Carbon Dioxide Removal Technologies, Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories (Additional guidance) — IPCC
item.description: The Panel agreed on the outline of the 2027 IPCC Methodology Report on Carbon Dioxide Removal Technologies, Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories (Additional guidance) at its 63rd Session held in Lima, Peru from 27-30 October 2025 (Decision IPCC-LXIII-6 ).

item.title: Media Contacts — IPCC
item.description: The IPCC welcomes media enquiries and supports media reports about its work and outcomes of its assessments. For further information, photos and video footage, please get in touch with the media contacts at the IPCC Secretariat and the Working Groups’ Technical Support Units.

item.title: IPCC opens registration for experts to review First Order Draft of Working Group I contribution to the Seventh Assessment Report — IPCC
item.description: IPCC opens registration for experts to review First Order Draft of Working Group I contribution to the Seventh Assessment Report — IPCC

item.title: Keynote address by the IPCC Chair Jim Skea at the Joint ODI-FAO event: “Just Transition for Agrifood Systems: Decent Livelihoods, Women’s Empowerment and Climate Resilience” — IPCC
item.description: Keynote address by the IPCC Chair Jim Skea at the Joint ODI-FAO event: “Just Transition for Agrifood Systems: Decent Livelihoods, Women’s Empowerment and Climate Resilience” — IPCC

item.title: Keynote address by the IPCC Chair, Jim Skea, at the World Climate Investment Summit — IPCC
item.description: Keynote address by the IPCC Chair, Jim Skea, at the World Climate Investment Summit — IPCC

item.title: IPCC Chair’s Remarks at the 18th Meeting of the Research Dialogue — IPCC
item.description: IPCC Chair’s Remarks at the 18th Meeting of the Research Dialogue — IPCC

item.title: IPCC at the 2026 June Climate Meetings — IPCC
item.description: IPCC at the 2026 June Climate Meetings — IPCC

item.title: Experts gather in Rome to discuss climate change impacts, adaptation and solutions for agriculture and food systems — IPCC
item.description: Experts gather in Rome to discuss climate change impacts, adaptation and solutions for agriculture and food systems — IPCC

item.title: IPCC News Comment — IPCC
item.description: IPCC News Comment — IPCC
